title: India Rising
context: UPA-2's policy paralysis grinds the economy, yet India's startup ecosystem quietly ignites. Mobile internet sits at 3% penetration. Foreign capital flows in despite corruption scandals. The seeds of a decade-long drama are being planted. This environment tends to reward companies building infrastructure or dominant consumer positions ahead of the curve.

title: Disruption and Cracks
context: Post-Jio India is unrecognisable. Data is effectively free. Digital payments surge after demonetisation. GST creates short-term pain. But cracks appear: the IL&FS crisis rattles NBFCs, global trade wars shadow markets, and some corporate facades begin to crumble. This environment tends to reward high-quality governance and severely penalizes fundamentally weak business models.

title: COVID Chaos and Digital Explosion
context: March 2020: Indian markets crash 40% in weeks. Then central banks print unprecedented money. Lockdowns make digital the only economy that works. EdTech and IT go parabolic. Banking braces for an NPA wave. Some companies will emerge from this unrecognisable — for better or worse. This environment tends to reward digital-first business models and those with ironclad balance sheets.

title: The Great Reckoning
context: January 2023: Hindenburg Research publishes a devastating 100-page report on Adani. ₹10 lakh crore of market cap evaporates in days. Byju's files for insolvency. EdTech winter arrives. Yet the Sensex crosses 80,000. Quality assets compound. Hubris gets punished. India's market is maturing. This environment tends to reward long-term quality and forces speculation out of the market.
